nodes.context: Mexican American street gang based in Oakwood, Venice, Los Angeles. Active since 1920s-1930s.
African-American street gang in Venice, founded mid-1970s.
Mexican-American street gang, rival of Venice 13.
Street gang, former rival turned ally of Venice 13.
Street gang, former rival turned ally of Venice 13.
Street gang in Santa Monica, rival of Venice 13.
Street gang, ally of Venice 13 per infobox.
Street gang, ally of Venice 13 per infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: It was amongst the first Mexican gangs to fight gang wars against African-American gangs, especially the local Venice Shoreline Crips which were founded in the mid-1970s.
edges.context: Venice 13 fought gang wars against Venice Shoreline Crips.

edges.evidence_quote: It is affiliated with the Sureños and the Mexican Mafia.
It is affiliated with the Sureños and the Mexican Mafia.
edges.context: Venice 13 is affiliated with Mexican Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: It is widely known for its deadly street violence with fellow Mexican-American gang, the Culver City Boys 13.
edges.context: Venice 13 has deadly street violence with Culver City Boys 13.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1994 Venice 13 and the Shorelines bargained for a cease fire, after a war that left 55 people dead and many more wounded.
edges.context: Venice 13 and Venice Shoreline Crips agreed to a cease fire in 1994.

edges.evidence_quote: Venice 13's main rivals were the Santa Monica 13 and Santa Monica 17th St gangs, however they both settled a truce and formed an alliance.
edges.context: Venice 13 and Santa Monica 13 were rivals but later formed an alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: Venice 13's main rivals were the Santa Monica 13 and Santa Monica 17th St gangs, however they both settled a truce and formed an alliance.
edges.context: Venice 13 and Santa Monica 17th St were rivals but later formed an alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: As well as 18th Street gang (particularly the 106th St in Inglewood), as well as the Grave Yard Gangsta Crips in Santa Monica and Culver City 13 in the Mar Vista neighborhood.
edges.context: Venice 13 has rivalry with 18th Street gang.

edges.evidence_quote: As well as 18th Street gang (particularly the 106th St in Inglewood), as well as the Grave Yard Gangsta Crips in Santa Monica and Culver City 13 in the Mar Vista neighborhood.

edges.evidence_quote: Venice 13 and the Culver City gang went as far as to set aside their own fight to unite against the Shorelines.
edges.context: Venice 13 and Culver City Boys 13 united against Venice Shoreline Crips.
